Items.Title: 模因星期一 | Meme Monday
Items.Description:
本·哈尔彭

发表于 5 月 22 日

模因星期一！

今天的封面图来自上周的话题。

低趣味的幽默会被模组否决。

一旦被暂停，Ben 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

取消暂停后，本将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，ben 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ben 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 ben 的操作：

降低 ben 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

Unflagging ben will restore default visibility to their posts. Unflagning ben 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Ben Halpern

Posted on May 22

   

Meme Monday!

Today's cover image comes from last week's thread.

DEV is an inclusive space! Humor in poor taste will be downvoted by mods.

Once suspended, ben will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ben will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ben will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ben is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag ben:

Make all posts by ben less visible

ben consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging ben will restore default visibility to their posts.
Items.Author: ben

Items.Title: 使用 react-tooltip 在 React 中创建响应式工具提示 | Creating responsive tooltips in React with react-tooltip
Items.Description:
添加一名作者

介绍

工具提示是一条简短的信息性消息，当用户与网页上的某些元素交互时出现。 工具提示的主要目的是在用户悬停或单击时向用户提供有关页面上的功能和元素的相关信息。

通常，工具提示显示为小框或弹出窗口，通常用于提供有关图标、按钮或应用程序中其他 UI 元素的附加信息。 根据应用程序的设计，工具提示的内容还可以包含各种多媒体，包括图像、视频和 GIF。

您可以通过以下 GIF 示例查看工具提示：

在本文中，我们将探讨如何在 React 应用程序中创建自定义工具提示组件，以及如何集成工具提示库，如 react-tooltip。

项目设置

您可以通过两种方式在 React 应用程序中使用工具提示，您可以从头开始创建自定义工具提示组件，也可以使用诸如 react-tooltip 之类的库。 我们将讨论如何设置这两种方法，但在深入研究细节之前，我们将快速设置一个简单的 React 应用程序来展示本文中的示例。
为此， refine.new是一个优秀的工具，我们可以使用它来快速创建一个新的 React CRUD 应用程序。

要开始，请前往refine.new/ ，向下滚动页面，然后单击Let's start按钮。

单击该按钮后，您将看到一个步进器组件，可让您选择和组合您喜欢的 React 平台、UI 框架、后端连接器和身份验证提供程序。

由于我们没有创建复杂的东西，我们将使用这些选项来设置我们的应用程序：

 Vite > Headless > Rest Api > No auth

完成所需步骤并单击“完成”按钮后，您将被重定向到精炼应用程序的预览页面。 在这里，您可以在构建和下载应用程序文件之前进行任何必要的调整。

根据您的喜好调整应用程序后，单击侧边栏上的Build & Download按钮下载应用程序。 如果您还没有，系统将提示您使用您的 Google 或 GitHub 个人资料登录。

登录后，将出现第二个弹出窗口，其中包含用于下载项目文件的 URL。

最后，解压缩下载的文件并在您选择的 IDE 中打开它。

现在我们已经设置了项目，最后一步是安装开发依赖项并启动开发服务器。 您可以通过在终端中执行以下命令来执行此操作：

这些命令将为您的项目安装必要的依赖项并启动开发服务器。 如果您的浏览器没有自动预览应用程序，您可以通过在浏览器中输入[http://127.0.0.1:5173](http://127.0.0.1:5173)手动导航到它。

当您使用 refine 创建一个新的无头项目时，您会注意到这些页面已经默认设置，但具有使用 refine Inferencer 包生成的占位符数据。

您可以通过访问文档找到有关此包的更多信息。

由于我们的应用程序页面由推理器填充，我们无法直接访问呈现的内容，因此我们无法将工具提示添加到特定元素，如表格上的create按钮。 但是，我们可以通过src/component目录中的Menu组件访问侧边栏导航。

当您创建 Headless refine 应用程序时，会在src/components目录中自动生成一个Layout组件。 该组件包装由推理器和包含侧边栏导航的Menu组件创建的页面。

虽然应用程序中的页面是自动生成的并且不可编辑，但Layout组件提供了对应用程序如何呈现给浏览器的某种程度的控制。

我们可以简单地清除Layout组件并创建一个简单的组件，我们可以使用它来为我们的示例呈现元素。 但是，由于 refine 生成的预定义页面模拟了真实世界的应用程序，因此我们的示例与实际应用程序中的样子很接近。

创建自定义工具提示组件

工具提示的结构并不像看起来那么复杂。 它由包含一些文本的单个元素组成，并且绝对定位到锚元素（其功能由工具提示解释的元素）或其父元素。 当悬停或单击锚元素时，工具提示的不透明度会增加。

如果我们要为侧边栏导航创建自定义工具提示，则Menu组件内的标记结构如下：

 <nav className="menu"> <ul> {menuItems.map((item) => ( <li key={item.key} className="tooltip_element"> <NavLink to={item.route}>{item.label}</NavLink> <span className="tooltip">navigate to {item.label}</span> </li> ))} </ul> </nav>;

我们在这里所做的是不言自明的； 我们只是在导航链接旁边添加了一个包含一些文本的span元素。

现在是样式：

 .tooltip_element { position: relative; } .tooltip { position: absolute; background-color: black; color: white; padding: 5px; border-radius: 5px; opacity: 0; transition: opacity 1s; top: 0; left: 80px; width: max-content; } .tooltip_element:hover .tooltip { opacity: 1; }

在这里，我们将工具提示绝对定位到导航链接。 最初，它的不透明度设置为0 ，只有当导航链接悬停时，它才会以不透明度1显示。 这就是创建简单工具提示所需的全部内容。

如果您保存进度并返回浏览器，您应该会看到类似于下面的 GIF 的内容。

截至目前，我们已将工具提示硬编码到我们的应用程序中，但这种方法对于更大、更复杂的实际应用程序并不实用。 为了使其具有可扩展性，我们可以为工具提示创建一个单独的组件，并通过 props 将文本和其他属性（如颜色、位置和背景颜色）传递给它。

要实现这一点，请在项目的src/component目录中创建一个新的tooltip文件夹，然后使用以下代码添加一个index.tsx文件：

 import * as React from "react"; export interface ITooltipProps { position: string; message: string; } export function Tooltip({ message, position }: ITooltipProps) { return ( <div> <span className={`tooltip tooltip-${position}`}>{message}</span> </div> ); }

在此代码块中，我们基本上在Menu组件中执行与之前相同的操作，但现在我们将内容和位置类动态传递给span元素。 位置类名将允许我们使用工具提示元素的top 、 right 、 bottom和left属性来定位工具提示。 我们需要为将传递给组件的每个位置创建样式：

 .tooltip-right { top: -5px; left: 105%; } .tooltip-left { top: -5px; right: 105%; } .tooltip-top { bottom: 105%; left: 0; } .tooltip-bottom { top: 105%; left: 0; }

现在，如果将工具提示组件导入菜单组件并将内容和所需位置作为道具传递，则在浏览器中查看应用程序时应该会看到类似的结果。

 import { useMenu } from "@refinedev/core"; import { NavLink } from "react-router-dom"; import { Tooltip } from "../tooltip"; export const Menu = () => { const { menuItems } = useMenu(); return ( <nav className="menu"> <ul> {menuItems.map((item) => ( <li key={item.key} className="tooltip_element"> <NavLink to={item.route}>{item.label}</NavLink> <Tooltip message={`route to ${item.label}`} position="bottom" /> </li> ))} </ul> </nav> ); };

我们的工具提示组件现在可以在整个应用程序中使用，但是，它缺少一些需要大量时间和精力才能实现的关键功能。 因此，建议使用像 react-tooltip 这样的工具提示库。

在接下来的章节中，我们将介绍和演示如何将 react-tooltip 集成到我们的应用程序中。

什么是 React 工具提示？

它允许开发人员通过提供一种简单且可自定义的方法来向任何 React 组件添加工具提示，从而创建动态且引人入胜的用户界面。

借助 react-tooltip，开发人员可以轻松控制应用程序中工具提示的内容、外观和行为。 总的来说，该包简化了工具提示的实现过程，为开发人员节省了时间和精力。

设置反应工具提示

要将 react-tooltip 合并到我们的应用程序中，我们需要先安装该包。 首先转到终端并执行以下命令：

 npm install react-tooltip or yarn add react-tooltip

接下来，导航到项目的index.tsx文件并添加以下 CSS 导入：

 import "react-tooltip/dist/react-tooltip.css";

此 CSS 文件包含反应工具提示在您的应用程序中正确呈现工具提示所需的所有必要样式。 将此文件包含在index.tsx或等效的顶级文件中至关重要； 否则，工具提示可能无法正确呈现。

我们将在以下部分探讨如何有效地做到这一点。

使用反应工具提示

有两种方法可以将组件绑定到锚元素：

  • 在元素上使用数据属性
  • 使用 ReactTooltip 组件和道具

使用数据属性
此方法需要通过特定数据属性（例如data-tooltip-content 、 data-tooltip-place和data-tooltip-id直接在锚元素上绑定和定义工具提示的内容和位置。

它被分配了一个与锚元素上的 ID 相对应的 ID。 而data-tooltip-content和data-tooltip-place分别用于设置工具提示的内容和位置。

要在我们的应用程序中使用此方法，我们首先将<Tooltip/>组件导入Menu组件，然后使用数据属性将工具提示的内容和位置传递给锚点元素，如下所示：

 import { Tooltip } from "react-tooltip"; export const Menu = () => { const { menuItems } = useMenu(); return ( <nav className="menu"> <ul> {menuItems.map((item) => ( <li key={item.key} className="tooltip_element"> <NavLink data-tooltip-id="my-tooltip" data-tooltip-content="Hello world!" data-tooltip-place="top" to={item.route} > {item.label} </NavLink> </li> ))} </ul> </nav> ); };

接下来，我们将在锚元素旁边定义<Tooltip>组件，并为其赋予与导航元素上的data-tooltip-id属性相同的 ID：

 <li key={item.key} className="tooltip_element"> <> <NavLink data-tooltip-id="my-tooltip" data-tooltip-content={`route to ${item.label}`} data-tooltip-place="top" to={item.route} > {item.label} </NavLink> <Tooltip id="my-tooltip" /> </> </li>;

这会将<Tooltip/>组件绑定到导航并在它们悬停时显示指定的内容。

面向认真的 Web 开发人员的开源企业应用程序平台

您可以在几秒钟内为您的项目创建量身定制的架构。 感觉就像触手可及地访问数以千计的项目模板，让您选择最适合您需要的模板！

使用 React 工具提示组件

与之前的方法不同，此技术使用 CSS 选择器将锚元素与工具提示组件绑定，而不是依赖数据属性。 它的功能是定位具有匹配选择器的锚元素并将工具提示附加到它们。

请注意， anchorSelect属性以点为前缀，这是必需的，因为它接受有效的 CSS 选择器。 如果我们改为在锚元素上设置 ID，我们将使用散列来开始选择器。

 <li key={item.key} className="tooltip_element"> <> <NavLink className="my-tooltip" to={item.route}> {item.label} </NavLink> <Tooltip anchorSelect=".my-tooltip" place="left"> route to {item.label} </Tooltip> </> </li>;

工具提示将同样呈现在浏览器上。

定位

在整篇文章中，我们一直在不同的示例中演示如何使用定位，因此您可能很好地理解它在低级和高级实现中的工作原理。

您可能很好奇为什么工具提示出现在锚元素的右侧而不是左侧。 这让我们想到了 react-tooltip 的另一个有用的特性：响应能力。 默认情况下，react-tooltip 会自行调整以适应您的设计，而不受限制。 在这种情况下，导航区域左侧已没有剩余空间。 因此，react-tooltip 在右侧而不是左侧呈现工具提示。

延迟

现在我们了解了 react-tooltip 的工作原理以及将其集成到项目中的各种方式，让我们来看看它的一些突出功能。 从延迟开始，此功能使您能够在锚元素悬停在工具提示出现或消失之前引入延迟。

工具提示延迟显示
它需要一个以毫秒为单位的数值来指定延迟的持续时间。

 <li key={item.key} className="tooltip_element"> <> <NavLink data-tooltip-id="my-tooltip" data-tooltip-content={`route to ${item.label}`} data-tooltip-place="top" data-tooltip-delay-show={1000} to={item.route} > {item.label} </NavLink> <Tooltip id="my-tooltip" /> </> </li>;

tooltip-delay-hide This attribute does the opposite of the former, as it adds a delay to when the tooltip disappears from the screen after the mouse leaves the anchor element. tooltip-delay-hide该属性与前者相反，因为它在鼠标离开锚点元素后工具提示从屏幕上消失时增加了延迟。



原文:

Author: David Omotayo

Introduction

A tooltip is a short and informative message that appears when a user interacts with certain elements on a webpage. The main objective of a tooltip is to provide users with relevant information about the features and elements on the page when they are hovered or clicked.

Typically, a tooltip is displayed as a small box or pop-up and is commonly used to offer additional information about an icon, button, or other UI elements in an application. The content of a tooltip can also consist of various multimedia, including images, videos, and GIFs, based on the application's design.

You can see a tooltip in action through the following GIF example:

In this article, we'll explore how to create a custom tooltip component in a React application, as well as integrating a tooltip library like react-tooltip.

Project setup

There are two ways you can use tooltips in your React applications, you either create a custom tooltip component from scratch or use a library such as react-tooltip. We'll go over how to set up both approaches, but before delving into the specifics, we'll quickly set up a simple React application to showcase the examples in this article.
To this end, refine.new serves as an excellent tool that we can use to rapidly create a new React CRUD application.

refine.new enables you to quickly bootstrap a new refine application, an open-source React-based, headless UI library for creating enterprise applications, within your browser with features such as preview, tweaking, and instant download.

To get started, head on to refine.new/, scroll down the page, and click on the Let's start button.

Upon clicking the button, you’ll be presented with a stepper component that let you choose and combine your preferred React platform, UI framework, backend connector, and auth provider.

Since we’re not creating something complex, we’ll use these options to set up our application: 

Vite > Headless > Rest Api > No auth

After completing the required steps and clicking the 'complete' button, you will be redirected to a preview page for your refine application. Here, you can make any necessary adjustments before building and downloading the application file.

Once you're done tweaking the application to your taste, click on the Build & Download button on the sidebar to download the application. If you haven't already, you will be prompted to sign in with your Google or GitHub profile.

After logging in, a second pop-up would appear with a URL for downloading the project file.

Finally, extract the downloaded file and open it in your IDE of choice.

Now that we have our project set up, the final step is to install the development dependencies and start the development server. you can do this by executing the commands below in your terminal:

These commands will install the necessary dependencies for your project and start the development server. If your browser doesn’t automatically preview the application, you can manually navigate to it by entering [http://127.0.0.1:5173](http://127.0.0.1:5173) in your browser.

refine was originally designed to provide a rapid way of bootstrapping enterprise-level React applications, complete with predefined pages. When you create a new headless project with refine, you’ll notice that these pages are already set up by default, but with placeholder data generated using the refine Inferencer package.

The refine inferencer package is a tool that automates the generation of CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) pages for resources in your refine application based on the data model. You can find more information about this package by visiting the documentation.

Since our application's pages are being populated by the inferencer, we don't have direct access to the rendered content, so we cannot add a tooltip to a specific element like the create button on the table. However, we can access the sidebar navigations via the Menu component in the src/component directory.

When you create a Headless refine application, a Layout component is automatically generated in the src/components directory. This component wraps the pages created by the inferencer and the Menu component, which contains the sidebar navigation.

While the pages in the applications are automatically generate and are not editable, the Layout component provides some level of control over how the application is rendered to the browser.

We could simply clear out the Layout component and create a simple component that we can use to render an element for our examples. However, since the predefined pages generated by refine simulate a real-world application, we have something close to what our examples may look like in an actual application.

Creating a custom tooltip component

The structure of a tooltip is not as complex as it may seem. It consists of a single element that contains some text and is positioned absolutely to the anchor element ( element whose functionality is explained by the tooltip) or its parent element. When the anchor element is hovered or clicked, the opacity of the tooltip is increased.

If we were to create a custom tooltip for the sidebar navigation, the markup inside the Menu component would be structured as follows:

<nav className="menu"> <ul> {menuItems.map((item) => ( <li key={item.key} className="tooltip_element"> <NavLink to={item.route}>{item.label}</NavLink> <span className="tooltip">navigate to {item.label}</span> </li> ))} </ul>
</nav>;

What we did here is self-explanatory; we simply added a span element containing some text next to the navigation links.

And now for the styles:

.tooltip_element { position: relative;
} .tooltip { position: absolute; background-color: black; color: white; padding: 5px; border-radius: 5px; opacity: 0; transition: opacity 1s; top: 0; left: 80px; width: max-content;
} .tooltip_element:hover .tooltip { opacity: 1;
}

Here, we’re positioning the tooltip absolutely to the navigation links. initially, its opacity is set to 0, and it becomes visible with an opacity of 1 only when the navigation links are hovered over. This is all that is needed to create a simple tooltip.

If you save your progress and go back to the browser, you should see something similar to the GIF below.

As of now, we have hardcoded the tooltip into our application, but this approach won't be practical for larger and more complex real-world applications. To make it scalable, we can create a separate component for the tooltip and pass the text and other properties such as color, position, and background color to it through props.

To implement this, create a new tooltip folder within the src/component directory of your project and then add a index.tsx file with the following code:

import * as React from "react"; export interface ITooltipProps { position: string; message: string;
} export function Tooltip({ message, position }: ITooltipProps) { return ( <div> <span className={`tooltip tooltip-${position}`}>{message}</span> </div> );
}

In this code block, we’re basically doing the same thing as before within the Menu component, but now we're passing the content and a position class to the span element dynamically. The position class name will allow us to position the tooltip using the top, right, bottom, and left properties of the tooltip element. We'll need to create styles for each position that will be passed to the component:

.tooltip-right { top: -5px; left: 105%;
} .tooltip-left { top: -5px; right: 105%;
} .tooltip-top { bottom: 105%; left: 0;
} .tooltip-bottom { top: 105%; left: 0;
}

Now, If you import the tooltip component into the Menu component and pass the content and desired position as props, you should see a similar result when you view your application in the browser.

import { useMenu } from "@refinedev/core";
import { NavLink } from "react-router-dom";
import { Tooltip } from "../tooltip"; export const Menu = () => { const { menuItems } = useMenu(); return ( <nav className="menu"> <ul> {menuItems.map((item) => ( <li key={item.key} className="tooltip_element"> <NavLink to={item.route}>{item.label}</NavLink> <Tooltip message={`route to ${item.label}`} position="bottom" /> </li> ))} </ul> </nav> );
};

Our tooltip component is now ready for use throughout the application, however, it's missing some crucial features that would require significant time and effort to implement. For this reason, it is recommended to use a tooltip library like react-tooltip.

In the following sections, we will introduce and demonstrate how to integrate react-tooltip into our application.

What is React-tooltip?

React tooltip is an open-source package for adding tooltips to react applications. It allows developers to create dynamic and engaging user interfaces by providing a simple and customizable approach to adding tooltips to any React component.

With react-tooltip, developers can easily control the content, appearance, and behavior of the tooltips in their applications. Overall, the package simplifies the process of implementing tooltips and saves time and effort for developers.

Setting up React-tooltip

To incorporate react-tooltip into our application, we need to install the package first. Start by going to the terminal and executing the following command:

npm install react-tooltip
or 
yarn add react-tooltip

Next, navigate to your project’s index.tsx file and add the following CSS import:

import "react-tooltip/dist/react-tooltip.css";

This CSS file contains all the necessary styles react-tooltip needs to properly render tooltips in your application. It is crucial to include this file in the index.tsx or an equivalent top-level file; otherwise, the tooltip may not render correctly.

react-tooltip has been successfully incorporated, we can now leverage its features throughout our application. We'll explore how to do so efficiently in the following sections.

Using React-tooltip

The react-tooltip package provides a <Tooltip/> component that can be bound to an anchor element and used to display element-specific information. There are two ways to bind the component to an anchor element:

  • Using data attributes on elements
  • Using the ReactTooltip component and props

Using data attributes
This method entails binding and defining the tooltip's content and position directly on anchor elements via specific data attributes, such as data-tooltip-content, data-tooltip-place, and data-tooltip-id.

The data-tooltip-id attribute is used to link the anchor element to the <Tooltip/> component. it is assigned an ID that corresponds to the ID on the anchor element. While the data-tooltip-content and data-tooltip-place are used to set the content and position of the tooltip, respectively.

To use this method in our application, we’ll start by importing the <Tooltip/> component into the Menu component and pass the content and position of the tooltip to the anchor element using the data attributes, as shown below:

import { Tooltip } from "react-tooltip"; export const Menu = () => { const { menuItems } = useMenu(); return ( <nav className="menu"> <ul> {menuItems.map((item) => ( <li key={item.key} className="tooltip_element"> <NavLink data-tooltip-id="my-tooltip" data-tooltip-content="Hello world!" data-tooltip-place="top" to={item.route} > {item.label} </NavLink> </li> ))} </ul> </nav> );
};

Next, we will define the <Tooltip> component next to the anchor element and give it the same ID as the data-tooltip-id attribute on the navigation element:

<li key={item.key} className="tooltip_element"> <> <NavLink data-tooltip-id="my-tooltip" data-tooltip-content={`route to ${item.label}`} data-tooltip-place="top" to={item.route} > {item.label} </NavLink> <Tooltip id="my-tooltip" /> </>
</li>;

This will bind the <Tooltip/> component to the navigations and display the specified content when they are hovered on.

Open-source enterprise application platform for serious web developers

refine.new enables you to create React-based, headless UI enterprise applications within your browser that you can preview, tweak and download instantly.

🚀 By visually combining options for your preferred ✨ React platform, ✨ UI framework, ✨ backend connector, and ✨ auth provider; you can create tailor-made architectures for your project in seconds. It feels like having access to thousands of project templates at your fingertips, allowing you to choose the one that best suits your needs!



Using React Tooltip component

In contrast to the previous method, this technique employs CSS selectors to bind the anchor element with the tooltip component instead of relying on data attributes. The tooltip component’s content is assigned to it as children, while other properties such as place and delay are defined using props.

<li key={item.key} className="tooltip_element"> <> <NavLink className="my-tooltip" to={item.route}> {item.label} </NavLink> <Tooltip anchorSelect=".my-tooltip" place="left"> route to {item.label} </Tooltip> </>
</li>;

The anchorSelect property takes a string that matches the CSS selector provided on the anchor element. Its function is to locate anchor elements with matching selectors and attach the tooltip to them.

Notice that the anchorSelect property is prefixed with a dot, which is necessary because it accepts valid CSS selectors. If we were to set an ID on the anchor element instead, we would use a hash to begin the selector.

<li key={item.key} className="tooltip_element"> <> <NavLink className="my-tooltip" to={item.route}> {item.label} </NavLink> <Tooltip anchorSelect=".my-tooltip" place="left"> route to {item.label} </Tooltip> </>
</li>;

The tooltip will render on the browser just the same.

Positioning

Throughout this article, we've consistently demonstrated how to use positioning in different examples, so you likely have a good understanding of how it works for both low-level and high-level implementations.

The place prop in the react-tooltip package is used to set the position of the tooltips and can be assigned one of four values that are similar to the position properties in CSS.

  • top: This positions the tooltip at the top of the anchor element

  • right: This positions the tooltip on the right side of the anchor element

  • bottom: This positions the tooltip at the top of the anchor element

  • left: This positions the tooltip on the left side of the anchor element

You may be curious about why the tooltip is appearing on the right of the anchor element rather than the left. This leads us to another useful feature of react-tooltip: responsiveness. By default, react-tooltip adjusts itself to fit your design, regardless of limitations. In this scenario, there is no more space left on the left side of the navigation area. Therefore, react-tooltip is rendering the tooltip on the right side instead of the left.

Delay

Now that we understand how react-tooltip works and the various ways you could integrate it into your project, let's take a look at some of its standout features. Beginning with Delays, this feature enables you to introduce a delay before the tooltip appears or disappears when the anchor element is hovered over.

tooltip-delay-show
The tooltip-delay-show attribute adds a delay before the tooltip appears when an anchor element is hovered on. It requires a number value in milliseconds to specify the duration of the delay.

<li key={item.key} className="tooltip_element"> <> <NavLink data-tooltip-id="my-tooltip" data-tooltip-content={`route to ${item.label}`} data-tooltip-place="top" data-tooltip-delay-show={1000} to={item.route} > {item.label} </NavLink> <Tooltip id="my-tooltip" /> </>
</li>;

tooltip-delay-hide This attribute does the opposite of the former, as it adds a delay to when the tooltip disappears from the screen after the mouse leaves the anchor element.
Items.Author: necatiozmen

Items.Title: 什么是开源，为什么它如此受欢迎？ | What is Open Source and Why is it so Goddamn Popular?
Items.Description:
今天我们将讨论开源 🔓。

您以前可能听说过这个词，但它的真正含义是什么？

从本质上讲，开源是指以协作和透明的方式开发的软件，其中的代码可供任何人免费访问、使用和修改。

简而言之，要记住的话是：

  • 合作开发👯‍♀️
  • 透明🔍
  • 免费🏷️

让我们深入研究一下..🧐

最著名的托管开源软件的网站称为Github 。

在下图中，您可以看到我自己的存储库，其中托管了一个名为Linkedin-Connecting-Bot 的开源项目。 存储库（在编码世界中称为“repo”）本质上是指托管特定项目的所有代码的文件夹。

确实，将其称为项目文档或代码文件夹会更直观，但原因是存储库包含所有以前版本的代码、所有历史记录和评论、所有贡献者信息、所有开发分支和所有文件元数据！

确实，一开始它可能看起来不像您预期​​的那样，但这确实是开源软件的样子。 这意味着并非 Github 上的所有内容都是开源的！ 换句话说，如果您愿意，您可以像构建私有项目一样构建开源项目！

现在，让我们回到这个问题——为什么它如此受欢迎？ 我觉得你现在可能已经有了一个想法，但这是造成这种情况的 5 个主要原因：

事实上，当我们一起建设时，我们会建设得更好🫶。

开放源代码允许开发人员协作并在彼此的工作基础上进行构建，从而开发出更好、更具创新性的产品。

通过共享他们的代码，开发人员可以相互学习，更快地发现和修复错误，并基于彼此的想法进行构建 - 这真是太酷了！

这种协作和社区驱动的开发导致了一些真正令人惊叹的项目，例如Linux 操作系统和Apache Web 服务器。

开源流行的另一个原因是它往往会带来更安全可靠的软件！

当代码公开并可供任何人检查时，更容易找到并修复漏洞 🚀

此外，由于社区不断努力改进和更新软件，它往往比专有软件更可靠和稳定，专有软件通常由一家公司或个人开发。

受益于开源的不仅仅是开发人员——用户也是如此。

开源软件意味着它可以免费使用，这对于可能没有资源投资昂贵的专有软件的个人、非营利组织和小型企业来说是一个巨大的优势。

总而言之，开源软件造福于整个社会！

例如，开源办公套件 LibreOffice 提供了许多与 Microsoft Office 相同的功能，但价格却不高！

开源项目通过促进协作和快速反馈循环来加速开发。

来自世界各地的开发人员可以贡献他们的专业知识、见解和改进，从而加快进度！

社区的集体智慧导致创新的解决方案和有效的问题解决。

因此，来自用户的持续反馈有助于及时识别和解决问题，从而提高软件质量。

开源项目因其可访问性、灵活性和社区驱动的特性而有可能加速采用。

当项目开源时，它对潜在用户更具吸引力，因为：

企业和个人都被它吸引，因为它提供了高质量的解决方案，而无需支付昂贵的许可费用。

所以你明白了——开源软件就是关于协作、透明和社区驱动的开发。

它导致：

总而言之，开源软件从根本上改变了科技领域的面貌，这就是它“如此受欢迎”的原因。

如果您还没有，即使是初学者，您也可以开始为开源做贡献——在接下来的几分钟内查看这篇关于如何开始的精彩文章！

谁知道呢，您可能只是下一件大事的一部分！ 这是一个寻找免费项目的好地方，您可以在其中做出贡献并有所作为！



原文:

tl;dr

💡 It create projects with a worldwide impact

🔒 It raises the bar for security

🔁 It accelerates development and feedback loops.

🧸 If the project is a product, it accelerates adoption

💰 Because everyone benefits from it

—

👋 Hey friends,

Today we're going to be talking about open source 🔓.

You might have heard this term before, but what does it really mean?

Essentially, open source refers to software that is developed in a collaborative and transparent way, where the code is freely available for anyone to access, use and modify.

In short, the words to remember are:

  • developed collaboratively 👯‍♀️
  • transparent 🔍
  • free 🏷️

👥 Okayyyy… I think I get it but, what does “open source software” actually look like?

Let’s dig into this a little bit.. 🧐

The most famous website hosting open-source software is called Github.

In the picture below, you see my own repository which hosts an open source project called Linkedin-Connecting-Bot. 👇

A repository (called "repo" in the coding world) essentially means the folder hosting all the code of a particular project.

It it true that calling it a project doc or code folder would have been more intuitive but the reason for that is that a repo contains all previous versions of code, all history & comments, all contributor information, all branches of development and all file metadata!

Now it's true that at first, it may not look like what you could have expected but this is literally what open-source software looks like. 😄

📝 Note: GitHub hosts both open-source software as well as private repositories! This means that not everything on Github is open source! In other words, you can get to build open-source projects just like you can build private projects if you so chose to!

Now, let's get back to the question - why is it so goddam popular? 🤔

I feel you may already have an idea by now but here are the 5 main reasons why this is the case:

1. Open Source builds projects with worldwide impact 🚀

Indeed, we build better when we build together 🫶.

Open-source allows developers to collaborate and build upon each other's work, leading to better and more innovative products.

By sharing their code, developers can learn from one another, find and fix bugs faster, and build upon each other's ideas - how freaking cool is that!

This kind of collaboration and community-driven development has led to some truly amazing projects, such as for example the Linux operating system and the Apache web server.

2. Enhanced Security 🔒

Another reason for the popularity of open source is that it often leads to more secure and reliable software!

When the code is open and available for anyone to inspect, it's easier to find and fix vulnerabilities 🚀

Additionally, because the community is constantly working to improve and update the software, it tends to be more reliable and stable than proprietary software, which is often developed by a single company or individual.

3. Everyone benefits from it 🎁

It's not just developers who benefit from open source - users do too.

Open source software means it is available for free, which is a huge advantage for individuals, non-profit organizations, and smaller businesses who may not have the resources to invest in expensive proprietary software.

All in all, open-source software benefits society as a whole!

For example, the open-source office suite LibreOffice provides many of the same features as Microsoft Office, but without the hefty price tag!

4. It nurtures development & feedback loops 🚀

Open-source projects accelerate development by fostering collaboration and rapid feedback loops.

Developers from around the world can contribute their expertise, insights, and improvements, resulting in faster progress!

The collective intelligence of the community leads to innovative solutions and efficient problem-solving.

As a result, continuous feedback from users helps identify and resolve issues promptly, improving the software's quality.

5. If the project is a product, it can accelerate adoption 💡

Open-source projects have the potential to accelerate adoption due to their accessibility, flexibility, and community-driven nature.

When a project is open-source, it becomes more attractive to potential users because:

📐 the availability of the source code empowers users to customize and adapt the software to suit their specific needs.

🤝 the community provides documentation, support forums, and contributes to the project's ecosystem, making it easier for newcomers to get started and find assistance when needed.

💸 the cost advantage of open source plays a significant role in driving adoption. Businesses and individuals are attracted to it because it offers high-quality solutions without the need for expensive licensing fees.

So there you have it - open-source software is all about collaboration, transparency, and community-driven development.

It leads to:

✅ impactful projects with

✅ higher security,

✅ more accessible software,

✅ better quality feedback loops and

✅ an accelerated product adoption 🚀

All in all, open-source software has radically changed the face of the tech landscape and this is why it is “so goddamn popular”.

If you haven't already, you can start contributing to open source even as a beginner - check this fantastic article on how to get started in the next few minutes!

Who knows, you might just be a part of the next big thing! 🔥

PS: If you're looking for a platform to discover projects aligned with your interests, language preference, and do it, whilst not spending a penny, visit quine.sh 🫶. It's a great space to find free projects where you can contribute and make a difference!
Items.Author: fernandezbaptiste

Items.Title: 在 Warp 终端上使用 Copilot CLI | Playing with Copilot CLI on top of Warp terminal
Items.Description:
我最近访问了新的 Copilot CLI，它基本上为您提供了 GitHub Copilot 的全部功能，但在您最喜欢的终端中……是时候尝试一下了！

截至今天，该工具处于候补名单阶段。 如果您还没有访问权限，您可以在 GitHub Next 上的Copilot for CLI页面上请求它。 需要有效的 Copilot 订阅。

设置

你得到访问权限了吗？ 耶！ 以下是如何开始：

  1. 将 CLI 安装为常规节点包： npm install -g @githubnext/github-copilot-cli
  2. 运行命令github-copilot-cli auth以使用您的 GitHub 帐户进行身份验证
  3. 使用eval "$(github-copilot-cli alias -- "$0")"设置别名

别名

为什么要设置别名？ 我不认为你想每次使用该工具时都输入github-copilot-cli ，就这么简单！

如果您在第 3 步运行命令，您将可以使用它们，但仅限于当前会话！ 要让它们全局可用，请确保在.bashrc或.zshrc文件中通过命令eval "$(github-copilot-cli alias -- "$0")" ，具体取决于您使用的 shell。

为了完整起见，除了方便的别名之外还有一个额外的原因，它在文档中进行了解释，简而言之，它使该工具更好地工作。

在所有这些解释之后，这是您将获得的别名列表：

  • ?? 测试运行

    由于我最近开始使用也有 AI 助手的Warp终端，我认为同时尝试两者会很有趣！

    这些天我们充斥着人工智能辅助工具，它们都很棒，但在某些时候，我们应该找出最适合我们每个人的工具。

    让我们用一个简单的方法来热身，摆脱node_modules文件夹的好方法。

    迅速的

    删除该项目的节点模块

    副驾驶 CLI

    大约一秒钟后，该工具建议了预期的rm -rf node_modules ，您可以在下面看到解释仍在进行中。

    又过了一秒钟，解释也到了。

    如果您想运行命令、修改查询或取消并返回到提示，是时候使用向上/向下箭头进行选择了。

    值得注意的是，在选择了第一个选项后，它仍然没有立即执行，你得到了大写 N 的已知 (y/N) 问题。

    起初，这让我以为按 Enter 时会自动选择“否”，但看起来并非如此。

    我按下y并执行了命令。 您可以在此处查看输出： app.warp.dev/block/Yy5mEuxoMJAoq6VScNqT ...

    经

    在 Warp 中对应的是# ，所以让我们用它来问 Warp 同样的问题！ 我看到的第一个区别是，只要您键入#就会出现 AI 面板。

    按 Enter 大约一秒钟后，命令出现在“建​​议的命令”下。

    使用Cmd+Enter命令不会执行，但会实际输入到终端中，因此您仍然可以在运行之前对其进行编辑。

    说到解释，您可以通过单击“解释这是如何工作的”按钮来请求它。

    将出现一个侧面板，再次按 Enter，您将看到深入的解释。

    细化建议

    毕竟它应该是一个建议，不一定是立即运行的命令。

    说到优化命令，Copilot CLI 使用了一种我不太喜欢的方法。 无法直接编辑命令，您只能更改查询并获得新建议。 我的意思是，它利用了更多的 AI 功能，但有时您只想直接编辑命令。

    但也许这只是我个人的看法。 请在评论中让我知道您最喜欢哪种方法，是通过 AI 优化命令还是能够直接编辑它。

    命令解释

    再一次，我们在这里谈论的是建议，所以总是欢迎解释。 同样，我们看到了两种不同的方法。

    尽管解释的深度不同！ 这次我对这两种方法都没有强烈的感觉，所以我再次征求你的意见。

    一个简短的命令

    在文章的开头，我提到您可能想要编辑.bashrc或.zshrc文件以使别名在全球范围内可用，但您该怎么做呢？ 去问AI吧！

    迅速的

    编辑默认 zsh 配置

    副驾驶 CLI

    起初，它建议nano ~/.zshrc这是一个好的开始，但我想改用 VS Code。 这次我选择了“修改查询”并要求使用 vscode。

    它使用code ~/.zshrc重新生成了建议，这正是我想要的。

    完整输出： app.warp.dev/block/mnMviqjfJE2WUsoEXfd4 ...

    经

    我的意思是，看起来这些人工智能工具没有订阅我的YouTube 频道，否则，他们会知道我使用 Visual Studio Code！

    一个棘手的

    让我们升级一点吧！

    迅速的

    在 content/blog 中找到所有图像并将它们添加到名为 blogImages 的 zip 中

    副驾驶 CLI

    同样，解释真的很短，但没有遗漏任何特别的东西。

    经

    这里的解释和往常一样更长，更“自然的语言”，就好像有人在给你解释一样。

    此命令在目录“content/blog”中查找所有扩展名为“.jpg”或“.png”的文件，并将输出通过管道传输到zip命令。 换句话说，此命令会创建一个名为“blogImages.zip”的 zip 文件，其中包含“content/blog”目录中的所有“.jpg”和“.png”文件。

    处理哈士奇错误

    在处理开源项目时，我注意到每次提交后都会收到此消息：

     hint: The '.husky/pre-commit' hook was ignored because it's not set as executable. hint: You can disable this warning with `git config advice.ignoredHook false`.

    迅速的

    我该如何修复此错误：提示：'.husky/pre-commit' 挂钩被忽略，因为它未设置为可执行文件。 提示：您可以使用git config advice.ignoredHook false禁用此警告。

    副驾驶 CLI

     ──────────────────── Command ──────────────────── chmod +x .husky/pre-commit ────────────────── Explanation ────────────────── ○ chmod is used to change the permissions of a file. ◆ +x adds the executable permission. ◆ .husky/pre-commit is the file whose permissions we want to change.

    经

    chmod +x .husky/pre-commit

    同样的命令，所以我要求解释一下：

    您收到的输出表明提交成功并创建了一个新文件test.txt 。 但是，有一条关于.husky/pre-commit挂钩未设置为可执行文件的警告消息。

    此警告消息与 Git 挂钩有关，Git 挂钩是在某些 Git 命令之前或之后自动运行的脚本。 要修复警告消息，您可以通过在终端中运行以下命令来使.husky/pre-commit挂钩可执行：

     chmod +x .husky/pre-commit

    此命令为.husky目录中的pre-commit文件设置可执行位，这允许 Git 在每次提交之前运行脚本。

    在使.husky/pre-commit挂钩可执行后，您可以像往常一样继续使用 Git。

    一个非常好的，这正是我想要的！

    用自然语言寻找东西

    迅速的

    帮我在这个文件夹的博客文章中找到 PII

    副驾驶 CLI

    我在这里很笼统，我没有具体说明 PII 是什么，也没有具体说明我想用它做什么。 经

    但是，我不知道那个正则表达式是什么，所以又到了寻求解释的时候了：

    这是一个使用grep实用程序递归地在/path/to/folder目录下的所有文件中搜索正则表达式模式的命令。

    这是命令的细分：

    • 此模式匹配三种类型的字符串：
    • 一个 9 位数字
    • 格式为XXX-XX-XXXX社会保险号
    • 格式为username@domain.com电子邮件地址
    • 因此，当您运行此命令时， grep会递归地搜索/path/to/folder目录下的所有文件，并打印出与正则表达式模式匹配的所有行。 正则表达式模式匹配任何包含 9 位数字、社会安全号码或电子邮件地址的行。

    现在这更有意义了！

    移动文件

    对于下一个测试，我实际上想更改一些文件。 我已经有了它们的 zip，所以让我们使用 AI 来建议我移动东西的命令！

    迅速的

    提取 tmp.zip 的内容并将其放入我的根目录中名为 temp 的新文件夹中

    副驾驶 CLI

     ──────────────────── Command ──────────────────── unzip tmp.zip -d ~/temp ────────────────── Explanation ────────────────── ○ unzip is used to extract files from a zip archive. ◆ tmp.zip is the name of the zip archive. ◆ -d ~/temp specifies that we want to extract the files to the ~/temp folder.

    好，易于！

    经

    unzip ~/Downloads/tmp.zip -d ~/temp

    不知道为什么它假设 zip 在下载中，但 Warp 再次让我手动编辑命令，所以我只修复路径，就是这样！

    匿名化一些数据

    现在我的临时文件夹中有一些文件，让我们尝试将它们匿名化！

    迅速的

    查找所有 PII 并将其匿名化

    副驾驶 CLI

     ──────────────────── Command ──────────────────── find . -name "*.py" | xargs sed -i "s/([0-9]{3}) [0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4}/(xxx) xxx-xxxx/g" ────────────────── Explanation ────────────────── ○ find is used to list files. ◆ -name "*.py" stipulates that we search for files ending in .py. ○ | xargs passes the list of files to the next command. ◆ sed is used to search and replace text in files. ◇ -i specifies that we want to replace the text in the files themselves. ◇ "s/([0-9]{3}) [0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4}/(xxx) xxx-xxxx/g" is the search and replace pattern. ▪ s means we want to search and replace. ▪ ([0-9]{3}) [0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4} is the search pattern. ▫ ([0-9]{3}) means we want to match three digits and capture them in a group. ▫ means we want to match a space. ▫ [0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4} means we want to match three digits, a dash, and four digits. ▪ (xxx) xxx-xxxx is the replacement pattern. ▫ (xxx) means we want to insert the string (xxx) into the text. ▫ means we want to insert a space. ▫ xxx-xxxx means we want to insert the string xxx-xxxx into the text. ▪ g means we want to replace all occurrences, not just the first one.

    这次 Copilot 做了一个大胆的假设，只找.py文件。 我用“修改查询”告诉他“文件在降价”，它用find . -name "*.md" 经

    这让我觉得提示不够清楚，所以我尝试改写一下：

    在此文件夹中查找所有 PII 并将它们匿名化

    find . -type f -exec sed -i 's/[0-9]\{3\}-[0-9]\{2\}-[0-9]\{4\}/XXX-XX-XXXX/g' {} +

    我故意对 PII 是什么含糊不清，但它开始变得有意义了。 让我们用更具体的提示再试一次：

    查找此文件夹中的所有电子邮件和电话号码并将其匿名化

    find . -type f -exec sed -i -E 's/[[:alnum:]+\.\_\-]+@[[:alnum:]+\.\_\-]+/[ANONYMIZED_EMAIL]/g; s/[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4}/[ANONYMIZED_PHONE]/g' {} +

    我运行它并... sed: RE error: illegal byte sequence （参见app.warp.dev/block/31KX08qIi09BN1PS6GtA ...）

    好吧好吧...让我们向 Warp AI 寻求解释：

    错误消息sed: RE error: illegal byte sequence表示sed遇到了它无法处理的字节序列。 当sed尝试处理包含非 ASCII 字符的文件或文件编码与当前语言环境不兼容时，可能会发生此错误。

    要修复此错误，您可以尝试将-i''选项添加到sed命令以在进行任何更改之前创建原始文件的备份。 此选项有助于避免文件编码和非 ASCII 字符的问题。

    这是更新的命令：

     find . -type f -exec sed -i'' -E 's/[[:alnum:]+\.\_\-]+@[[:alnum:]+\.\_\-]+/[ANONYMIZED_EMAIL]/g; s/[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4}/[ANONYMIZED_PHONE]/g' {} +

    此命令应在进行任何更改之前创建每个文件的备份，并将所有电子邮件地址和电话号码分别替换为[ANONYMIZED_EMAIL]和[ANONYMIZED_PHONE] 。

    如果错误仍然存​​在，您可以尝试使用-e选项明确指定文件编码。 例如文件编码为UTF-8，可以使用如下命令：

     find . -type f -exec sed -i'' -e 's/[[:alnum:]+\.\_\-]+@[[:alnum:]+\.\_\-]+/[ANONYMIZED_EMAIL]/g; s/[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4}/[ANONYMIZED_PHONE]/g' {} +

    此命令应分别用[ANONYMIZED_EMAIL]和[ANONYMIZED_PHONE]替换所有电子邮件地址和电话号码，同时正确处理非 ASCII 字符。

    那里有很多上下文，但实际上第一行已经让我意识到那个文件夹中也有图像……是时候出现一个新提示，将搜索范围限制为仅降价文件！

    在此文件夹中的降价文件中查找所有电子邮件和电话号码，并将它们匿名化

    这次我得到了一个工作命令：

     find . -name "*.md" -type f -exec sed -E -i '' 's/[[:alnum:]+\.\_\-]+@[[:alnum:]+\.\_\-]+/[email]/g; s/\b\d{3}[-.]?\d{3}[-.]?\d{4}\b/[phone]/g' {} +

    获胜者是...

    一定有赢家吗？

    这两种工具都非常快速和准确，但用户体验略有不同。

    我个人对于让 AI 在不先检查命令的情况下在我的机器上运行命令仍然不太有信心，所以我喜欢 Warp 让我打开一个聊天室，在那里我可以与 AI 讨论命令并寻求解释，而 Copilot只是给我一个命令的简短解释，仅此而已。

    我还必须提到， Warp实际上是一个完整的终端，因此您可以获得更多的交互和功能，而 Copilot 只是一个 CLI 工具……因此您可以从两者中受益！

    由于无论如何我都在使用 Warp，所以只需使用#或??启动命令即可。 取决于我正在寻找的人工智能体验，我已经准备好了！

    你呢？ 您尝试过这些工具吗？ 你对他们有什么经验？ 在评论中让我知道！

    感谢您阅读本文，希望您觉得它有趣！

    我最近启动了我的 Discord 服务器来讨论开源和 Web 开发，欢迎加入： discord.gg/bqwyEa6We6

    你喜欢我的内容吗？ 您可以考虑订阅我的 YouTube 频道！ 这对我来说意义重大❤️ 你可以在这里找到它：



原文:

I recently got access to the new Copilot CLI, which basically gives you the full power of GitHub Copilot but in your favourite terminal so... time to give it a try!

As of today, the tool is in the waitlist stage. If you don't have access yet, you can request it on the Copilot for CLI page on GitHub Next. An active Copilot subscription is required.

Setup

Did you get access? Yaay! Here's how to get started:

  1. Install the CLI as a regular node package: npm install -g @githubnext/github-copilot-cli
  2. Run the command github-copilot-cli auth to authenticate with your GitHub account
  3. Setup the aliases with eval "$(github-copilot-cli alias -- "$0")"

Aliases

Why set up aliases? I don't think you want to type github-copilot-cli every time you want to use the tool, as easy as that!

If you ran the command on step 3 you will have them available, but only in the current session! To have them globally available make sure to past the command eval "$(github-copilot-cli alias -- "$0")" in your .bashrc or .zshrc file, depending on the shell you're using.

For completeness, there's an extra reason besides having convenient aliases and it's explained in the documentation, in short, it makes the tool work better.

After all this explanation, here's the list of aliases you'll get:

  • ??: Ask for a generic shell command
  • git?: Ask for a git command
  • gh?: Ask for a GitHub CLI command

Test run

Since I recently started using the Warp terminal which also has an AI assistant, I thought it would be fun to try both at the same time!

These days we're flooded with AI-assisted tools, and they're all great, but at some point, we should find out which one works best for each one of us.

Let's warm up with an easy one, the good old getting rid of the node_modules folder.

Prompt

delete node modules of this project

Copilot CLI

After about a second the tool suggested the expected rm -rf node_modules, and you can see below the explanation was still pending.

One more second later and also the explanation arrived.

It's time to select with the up/down arrows if you want to run the command, revise the query or cancel and get back to the prompt.

It's worth noting that after selecting the first option it's still not immediately executed and you get the known (y/N) question with capital N.

At first, this made me think "No" was automatically selected when pressing Enter but looks like it's not the case.

I pressed y and the command was executed. You can see the output here: app.warp.dev/block/Yy5mEuxoMJAoq6VScNqT…

Warp

The ?? counterpart in Warp is #, so let's use it to ask Warp the same question! The first difference I see is that as soon as you type # the AI panel appears.

Press Enter and after about a second the command appears under "Suggested command".

With Cmd+Enter the command is not executed but actually put into the terminal, so you can still edit it before running it.

Speaking of the explanation, you can request it by clicking on the "Explain how this works" button.

A side panel will appear and by pressing Enter again you will see the in-depth explanation.

Refining the suggestion

AI tools are far from being perfect, you would not let them run commands by themselves without checking them first and in fact, both tools we tried here have a double step for confirmation and let you refine the command before running it.

It's supposed to be a suggestion after all, not necessarily a command to immediately run.

Speaking of refining the command, Copilot CLI uses an approach that I don't really like. It's not possible to edit the command directly, you can only change the query and get a new suggestion. I mean, it leverages even more the AI power but sometimes you just want to edit the command directly.

But maybe it's just my personal opinion there. Please let me know in the comments which approach you like the most, between having a refined command by the AI or being able to edit it directly.

Command explanation

Once more, we're talking about suggestions here, so an explanation is always welcome. And again, we see two different approaches.

Copilot CLI gives you the explanation as soon as the suggestion is ready, while Warp gives you the command first and then you can request the explanation manually, if you want.

The explanation isn't at the same depth though! Copilot arrives faster but with a really concise and short explanation, while Warp requires an extra user interaction but goes way more in-depth.

This time I don't have a strong feeling about either approach so I'm once more asking for your opinion.

A short command

At the beginning of the article I mentioned you might want to edit your .bashrc or .zshrc file to have the aliases available globally, but how can you do that? Let's ask the AI!

(well, you need it already up and running before asking, but you get the point!)

Prompt

edit default zsh config

Copilot CLI

At first, it suggested nano ~/.zshrc which is a good start, but I wanted to use VS Code instead. This time I went for "Revise query" and asked for vscode instead.

It regenerated the suggestion with code ~/.zshrc which is exactly what I wanted.

Full output: app.warp.dev/block/mnMviqjfJE2WUsoEXfd4…

Warp

The same goes for Warp, the first suggestion was nano ~/.zshrc.

I mean, looks like these Artificial Intelligence tools are not subscribed to my YouTube channel, otherwise, they would know I use Visual Studio Code!

A tricky one

Let's level up a little bit!

Prompt

find all images inside content/blog and add them to a zip called blogImages

Copilot CLI

Copilot only includes png images and passes them to the zip command with the well-known xargs.

find content/blog -name "*.png" | xargs zip blogImages

Again, the explanation is really really short but isn't missing anything in particular.

Warp

Warp includes both png and jpg images and passes them to the zip command with a zip-specific flag that is -@.

find content/blog -name "*.jpg" -o -name "*.png" | zip blogImages -@

The explanation here as usual is longer and with more "natural language", as if a human was explaining it to you.

This command finds all files with the extension ".jpg" or ".png" in the directory "content/blog" and pipes the output to the zip command. The zip command creates a zip file named "blogImages.zip" and adds the files from the standard input (indicated by the -@ option).

In other words, this command creates a zip file named "blogImages.zip" containing all the ".jpg" and ".png" files in the "content/blog" directory.

Dealing with a husky error

When working on an Open Source project I noticed I had this message after each commit:

hint: The '.husky/pre-commit' hook was ignored because it's not set as executable.
hint: You can disable this warning with `git config advice.ignoredHook false`.

Prompt

how can I fix this error: hint: The '.husky/pre-commit' hook was ignored because it's not set as executable. hint: You can disable this warning with git config advice.ignoredHook false.

Copilot CLI 

 ──────────────────── Command ──────────────────── chmod +x .husky/pre-commit ────────────────── Explanation ────────────────── ○ chmod is used to change the permissions of a file.
  ◆ +x adds the executable permission.
  ◆ .husky/pre-commit is the file whose permissions we want to change.

Warp 

chmod +x .husky/pre-commit

Same command, so I asked to explain it:

The output you received indicates that the commit was successful and a new file test.txt was created. However, there is a warning message about the .husky/pre-commit hook not being set as executable.

This warning message is related to Git hooks, which are scripts that run automatically before or after certain Git commands. The .husky/pre-commit hook is a pre-commit hook that is used by the Husky package to run scripts before each commit.

To fix the warning message, you can make the .husky/pre-commit hook executable by running the following command in your terminal:

chmod +x .husky/pre-commit

This command sets the executable bit for the pre-commit file in the .husky directory, which allows Git to run the script before each commit.

After making the .husky/pre-commit hook executable, you can continue working with Git as usual.

A really good one, it was exactly what I was looking for!

Looking for something in natural language

Prompt

help me find PII in the blog articles in this folder

Copilot CLI

I've been quite generic here, I didn't specify what PII is and I didn't specify what I want to do with it. Copilot suggested a grep command that looks for email, phone or address in all the .md files in the current directory which makes sense.

 ──────────────────── Command ──────────────────── for file in *.md; do grep -E "(email|phone|address)" $file;
done ────────────────── Explanation ────────────────── ○ The for loop iterates over a list of items and executes its body for each, using the loop variable $file. ◆ The list of items is *.md which means all files ending in .md in the current directory.
○ The loop body executes one command for each file: ◆ grep is used to search for a pattern in a file. ◇ -E means we want to use extended regular expressions. ◇ "(email|phone|address)" is the pattern we search for. ◇ $file is the file we search in.

Warp

Warp gave me this long regex and ended up with path/to/folder which is not exactly I wanted, but I can just accept the command and change the path manually.

grep -r -E "\b([0-9]{9}|[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{4}|[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,})\b" /path/to/folder

However, I had no idea what that regex was so it's once again time to ask for an explanation:

This is a command that uses the grep utility to search for a regular expression pattern in all files under the /path/to/folder directory recursively.

Here's a breakdown of the command:

  • grep is a command-line utility that searches for patterns in files.
  • -r is an option that tells grep to search recursively in all subdirectories under the specified directory.
  • -E is an option that tells grep to use extended regular expressions.
  • "\b([0-9]{9}|[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{4}|[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,})\b" is the regular expression pattern that grep searches for. This pattern matches three types of strings:
  • A 9-digit number
  • A social security number in the format XXX-XX-XXXX
  • An email address in the format username@domain.com
  • /path/to/folder is the directory where grep starts searching for files.

So, when you run this command, grep searches for all files under the /path/to/folder directory recursively and prints out all lines that match the regular expression pattern. The regular expression pattern matches any line that contains a 9-digit number, a social security number, or an email address.

This makes much more sense now!

Moving files around

For the next test I wanted to actually change some files. I already have a zip with them so let's use the AIs to suggest me the commands to move things around!

Prompt

Extract the content of tmp.zip and put it in a new folder called temp in my root directory

Copilot CLI 

 ──────────────────── Command ──────────────────── unzip tmp.zip -d ~/temp ────────────────── Explanation ────────────────── ○ unzip is used to extract files from a zip archive. ◆ tmp.zip is the name of the zip archive. ◆ -d ~/temp specifies that we want to extract the files to the ~/temp folder.

Nice and easy!

Warp 

unzip ~/Downloads/tmp.zip -d ~/temp

Not sure why it assumed the zip was inside download, but again Warp lets me manually edit the command so I just fix the path and that's it!

Anonymize some data

Now that I've got some files in my temp folder, let's try to anonymize them!

Prompt

Find all PII and anonymize them

Copilot CLI 

 ──────────────────── Command ──────────────────── find . -name "*.py" | xargs sed -i "s/([0-9]{3}) [0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4}/(xxx) xxx-xxxx/g" ────────────────── Explanation ────────────────── ○ find is used to list files. ◆ -name "*.py" stipulates that we search for files ending in .py.
○ | xargs passes the list of files to the next command. ◆ sed is used to search and replace text in files. ◇ -i specifies that we want to replace the text in the files themselves. ◇ "s/([0-9]{3}) [0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4}/(xxx) xxx-xxxx/g" is the search and replace pattern. ▪ s means we want to search and replace. ▪ ([0-9]{3}) [0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4} is the search pattern. ▫ ([0-9]{3}) means we want to match three digits and capture them in a group. ▫ means we want to match a space. ▫ [0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4} means we want to match three digits, a dash, and four digits. ▪ (xxx) xxx-xxxx is the replacement pattern. ▫ (xxx) means we want to insert the string (xxx) into the text.
        ▫   means we want to insert a space.
        ▫ xxx-xxxx means we want to insert the string xxx-xxxx into the text.
      ▪ g means we want to replace all occurrences, not just the first one.

This time Copilot did a bold assumption, only looking for .py files. I used "Revise Query" to tell him "Files are in markdown" and it updated the first command with find . -name "*.md".

Warp

Warp's take is consistent to the previous one in using a regex for the search, but by using grep it's not actually changing the files, just printing the lines that match the regex.

grep -rEIi '([0-9]{3}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{4}|[0-9]{3}.[0-9]{2}.[0-9]{4}|[0-9]{9})' . | sed -E 's/([0-9]{3}-[0-9]{2}-)[0-9]{4}/\1XXXX/g; s/([0-9]{3}.[0-9]{2}.)[0-9]{4}/\1XXXX/g; s/([0-9]{9})/XXXXXXXXX/g'

This makes me think the prompt wasn't clear enough, so I try to rephrase it:

Find all PII in this folder and anonymize them in place

find . -type f -exec sed -i 's/[0-9]\{3\}-[0-9]\{2\}-[0-9]\{4\}/XXX-XX-XXXX/g' {} +

I was deliberately vague about what a PII is but it starts to make sense. Let's give it another try with a more specific prompt:

Find all emails and phone numbers in this folder and anonymize them in place

find . -type f -exec sed -i -E 's/[[:alnum:]+\.\_\-]+@[[:alnum:]+\.\_\-]+/[ANONYMIZED_EMAIL]/g; s/[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4}/[ANONYMIZED_PHONE]/g' {} +

I ran it and... sed: RE error: illegal byte sequence (see app.warp.dev/block/31KX08qIi09BN1PS6GtA…)

Well well well... let's ask Warp AI for an explanation:

The error message sed: RE error: illegal byte sequence indicates that sed encountered a byte sequence that it cannot handle. This error can occur when sed tries to process a file that contains non-ASCII characters or when the file encoding is not compatible with the current locale.

To fix this error, you can try adding the -i'' option to the sed command to create a backup of the original file before making any changes. This option can help avoid issues with file encoding and non-ASCII characters.

Here's the updated command:

find . -type f -exec sed -i'' -E 's/[[:alnum:]+\.\_\-]+@[[:alnum:]+\.\_\-]+/[ANONYMIZED_EMAIL]/g; s/[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4}/[ANONYMIZED_PHONE]/g' {} +

This command should create a backup of each file before making any changes and replace all email addresses and phone numbers with [ANONYMIZED_EMAIL] and [ANONYMIZED_PHONE], respectively.

If the error persists, you can try specifying the file encoding explicitly using the -e option. For example, if the file encoding is UTF-8, you can use the following command:

find . -type f -exec sed -i'' -e 's/[[:alnum:]+\.\_\-]+@[[:alnum:]+\.\_\-]+/[ANONYMIZED_EMAIL]/g; s/[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4}/[ANONYMIZED_PHONE]/g' {} +

This command should replace all email addresses and phone numbers with [ANONYMIZED_EMAIL] and [ANONYMIZED_PHONE], respectively, while handling non-ASCII characters correctly.

A lot of context there, but actually the first line already made me realize that there are also images in that folder... time for a new prompt to restrict the scope of the search to markdown files only!

Find all emails and phone numbers in markdown files in this folder and anonymize them in place

And this time I got a working command:

find . -name "*.md" -type f -exec sed -E -i '' 's/[[:alnum:]+\.\_\-]+@[[:alnum:]+\.\_\-]+/[email]/g; s/\b\d{3}[-.]?\d{3}[-.]?\d{4}\b/[phone]/g' {} +

The winner is...

There has to be a winner?

Both tools are really fast and accurate but have a slightly different user experience.

I'm personally still not too confident in letting an AI run commands on my machine without checking them first, so I like that Warp lets me open a chat where I can discuss with the AI about the command and ask for an explanation, whereas Copilot just gives me a brief explanation of the command and that's it.

I also have to mention that Warp is actually an entire terminal so you can have way more interactions and features while Copilot is just a CLI tool... so you can benefit from both!

Since I'm using Warp anyway, it's just a matter of starting the command with either # or ?? depending on the AI experience I'm looking for and I'm already good to go!

What about you? Have you tried any of these tools? What's your experience with them? Let me know in the comments!

Thanks for reading this article, I hope you found it interesting!

I recently launched my Discord server to talk about Open Source and Web Development, feel free to join: discord.gg/bqwyEa6We6

Do you like my content? You might consider subscribing to my YouTube channel! It means a lot to me ❤️ You can find it here:
Items.Author: balastrong

Items.Title: 俄罗斯方块推荐语录 | Tetris Testimonial Quotes
Items.Description:
马兹·斯托曼

发表于 5 月 23 日

昨晚参加了本周的Codepen 挑战赛，玩得很开心。

我对这个话题一无所知——推荐语录——但后来我注意到我妻子在一个古老的 GameBoy 上玩俄罗斯方块，完全被吸引了😁

我启动了一个 7 列 14 行的CSS 网格。 真正的俄罗斯方块游戏有 10×20，但在移动设备上就显得太局促了。

我添加了颜色，以及一堆跨越列和行的微类：

 .cs1 { grid-column: span 1; } .cs2 { grid-column: span 2; } .cs3 { grid-column: span 3; } .cs4 { grid-column: span 4; } .rs2 { grid-row: span 2; } .rs3 { grid-row: span 3; } .rs4 { grid-row: span 4; }

然后——在标记中——只是一堆<b>标签（用于b锁！）：

 <b class="rs4 blue"></b> <b class="rs3 cyan"></b> <b class="cs1 cyan"></b> <b class="cs4 yellow"> <!-- etc. -->

对于引号，我使用了<q>和<cite>标签，偶尔为垂直文本添加不同的writing-mode ：

 .vtxt { text-orientation: mixed; writing-mode: vertical-rl; }

所有font-size都使用clamp ，所以它们可以很好地缩放，如下所示：

 q { font-size: clamp(0.875rem, -0.2045rem + 5.3977vw, 3.25rem); }

另一种方法是使用其中一个container单元，例如cqi 。

一旦暂停，madsstoumann 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，madsstoumann 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，madsstoumann 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 madsstoumann 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 madsstoumann 的方法：

降低 madsstoumann 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 madsstoumann 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Mads Stoumann

Posted on May 23

   

Had a bit of fun last night with this week's Codepen Challenge.

I had no ideas for the topic — Testimonial Quotes — but then I noticed my wife playing Tetris on an ancient GameBoy, completely absorbed 😁

I fired up a CSS Grid with 7 columns and 14 rows. A real Tetris game has 10×20, but it would be too cramped on mobile devices.

I added colors, and a bunch of micro-classes that span columns and rows:

.cs1 { grid-column: span 1; }
.cs2 { grid-column: span 2; }
.cs3 { grid-column: span 3; }
.cs4 { grid-column: span 4; }
.rs2 { grid-row: span 2; }
.rs3 { grid-row: span 3; }
.rs4 { grid-row: span 4; }

And then — in the markup — just a bunch of <b>-tags (for block!):

<b class="rs4 blue"></b>
<b class="rs3 cyan"></b>
<b class="cs1 cyan"></b>
<b class="cs4 yellow">
<!-- etc. -->

For the quotes, I used <q> and <cite>-tags, occasionally adding a different writing-mode for vertical text:

.vtxt { text-orientation: mixed; writing-mode: vertical-rl;
}

All font-sizes are using clamp, so they scale nicely, as in:

q { font-size: clamp(0.875rem, -0.2045rem + 5.3977vw, 3.25rem);
}

Another way of doing that, would be with one of the container-units, such as cqi.

ChapGPT helped me write the testimonials, and the result is here — hope you like it:

Once suspended, madsstoumann will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, madsstoumann will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by madsstoumann will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If madsstoumann is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag madsstoumann:

Make all posts by madsstoumann less visible

madsstoumann consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging madsstoumann will restore default visibility to their posts.
